title: Criminal Case No. 1000711-61.2022.4.01.4200
text:
In this case, the defendant  was charged with exploiting vulnerable, hearing-impaired Venezuelan immigrants in Boa Vista, Brazil, from late 2015 through November 2017. The defendant enticed  individuals from Venezuela through social media, offering false promises of employment. Once the victims arrived in Brazil, the defendant confiscated their identification documents and coerced them into begging in public spaces, such as banks and hospitals. The victims, forced to endure degrading work conditions and subject to physical and psychological abuse, were made to hand over all earnings to the accused. The investigation, carried out by the Organized Crime Task Force, uncovered evi de nce of forced labor and ensl avement in properties rented  by the  accused , where the victims were kept under conditions of servitude and debt bondage. 

The defendant was ultimately charged with crimes of human trafficking and reduction to conditions akin to slavery, as specified in Brazil’s Penal Code, Articles 149 and 149-A. The court found him guilty and sentenced him to 10 years, 6 months, and 2 days of imprisonment. Additionally, he received a fine and was mandated to begin his sentence in a high-security prison.
